title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
text: MexicoDataAPI package provides unified interface access open data World Bank API Nager.Date API, focus Mexico. allows users retrieve --date information topics economic indicators, population figures, literacy rates, unemployment levels, official public holidays. addition API-access functions, package includes set curated datasets related Mexico. cover areas air quality monitoring, state-level income surveys, postal abbreviations, election results, regional forest classification. MexicoDataAPI intended support users working data related Mexico integrating international API sources selected datasets national academic origins, single R package.

title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
text: MexicoDataAPI package provides several core functions access real-time structured information Mexico public APIs World Bank API Nager.Date API. list main functions included package: get_mexico_cpi(): Get Mexico’s Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) World Bank get_mexico_gdp(): Get Mexico’s GDP (Current US$) World Bank get_mexico_holidays(): Get official public holidays Mexico given year, e.g., get_mexico_holidays(2025). get_mexico_life_expectancy(): Get Mexico’s Life Expectancy World Bank get_mexico_literacy_rate(): Get Mexico’s Literacy Rate (Age 15+) World Bank get_mexico_population(): Get Mexico’s Population (Total) World Bank get_mexico_unemployment(): Get Mexico’s Unemployment Rate (%) World Bank view_datasets_MexicoDataAPI(): Lists curated datasets included MexicoDataAPI package functions allow users access high-quality structured information Mexico, can combined tools like dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2 support wide range data analysis visualization tasks. following sections, ’ll find examples work MexicoDataAPI practical scenarios.

what: Mexico’s GDP (Current US$) from World Bank 2022 - 2017
title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
code: mexico_gdp <- head(get_mexico_gdp())  print(mexico_gdp) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   indicator         country  year   value value_label       #>   <chr>             <chr>   <int>   <dbl> <chr>             #> 1 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2022 1.47e12 1,466,934,724,243 #> 2 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2021 1.32e12 1,316,569,466,834 #> 3 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2020 1.12e12 1,121,064,767,169 #> 4 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2019 1.30e12 1,304,106,204,006 #> 5 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2018 1.26e12 1,256,300,182,984 #> 6 GDP (current US$) Mexico   2017 1.19e12 1,190,721,475,853

what: Mexico’s Life Expectancy from World Bank 2022 - 2017
title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
code: life_expectancy <- head(get_mexico_life_expectancy())  print(life_expectancy) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   indicator                               country  year value #>   <chr>                                   <chr>   <int> <dbl> #> 1 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2022  74.0 #> 2 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2021  69.8 #> 3 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2020  70.4 #> 4 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2019  74.5 #> 5 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2018  74.3 #> 6 Life expectancy at birth, total (years) Mexico   2017  74.3

title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
code: mexico_population <- head(get_mexico_population())  print(mexico_population) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   indicator         country  year     value value_label #>   <chr>             <chr>   <int>     <int> <chr>       #> 1 Population, total Mexico   2022 128613117 128,613,117 #> 2 Population, total Mexico   2021 127648148 127,648,148 #> 3 Population, total Mexico   2020 126799054 126,799,054 #> 4 Population, total Mexico   2019 125762982 125,762,982 #> 5 Population, total Mexico   2018 124573711 124,573,711 #> 6 Population, total Mexico   2017 123400057 123,400,057

what: Average Household Income by Education Level (2008)
title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
code: # Summary of average income by education level avg_income_by_education <- mex_income_2008_tbl_df %>%   group_by(education) %>%   summarise(avg_income = mean(income, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%   arrange(desc(avg_income))  # Plot ggplot(avg_income_by_education, aes(x = reorder(education, avg_income), y = avg_income)) +   geom_col(fill = "#0072B2") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Average Household Income by Education Level (2008)",     x = "Education Level",     y = "Average Income (MXN)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
text: dataset MexicoDataAPI labeled suffix indicate structure type: _df: standard data frame. _tbl_df: tibble data frame object. _chr: character object.

title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
text: addition API access functions, MexicoDataAPI provides several preloaded datasets related Mexico’s environment, demographics, public data. featured examples: mexico_elections_df: Data frame containing subset 2012 Mexico Elections Panel Study. mex_income_2016_tbl_df: Tibble containing household-level income data associated demographic characteristics 2016 ENIGH (Household Income Expenditure Survey). mexico_abb_chr: Character vector containing official two- three-letter postal abbreviations 32 federal entities Mexico.

title: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets
text: MexicoDataAPI package provides comprehensive interface access open data Mexico RESTful APIs curated datasets. includes functions retrieve real-time information World Bank API Nager.Date API, covering topics population, GDP, CPI, life expectancy, literacy, unemployment, official public holidays. addition, package offers curated datasets related air quality monitoring stations, pollution zones, state-level income surveys 2008 2016, postal abbreviations, election studies, ecological data Chiapas dry forests. Together, resources support research, teaching, analysis focused Mexico’s economic, environmental, sociopolitical landscape.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Renzo Caceres Rossi. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

what: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
title: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
text: Retrieves selected, essential information Mexico country full name. data retrieved REST Countries API. See API documentation https://restcountries.com/. Example API usage: https://restcountries.com/v3.1/name/mexico?fullText=true.

title: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
text: name Full country name (common official). example: "Mexico", "Peru", "France".

title: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
text: data frame 8 columns: name_common: Common name country. name_official: Official name country. capital: Capital city. region: Geographic region. subregion: Subregion. population: Total population. area: Total area square kilometers. languages: Official languages, separated commas.

title: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info
text: function returns readable details country's common official name, capital, region, subregion, population, area, official languages. function sends GET request REST Countries API. request successful (HTTP 200), parses JSON extracts key fields. country found error, function returns NULL user-friendly message.

title: Get Key Country Information from the REST Countries API — get_country_info

what: Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
title: Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
text: Retrieves comprehensive country information Mexico REST Countries API. function fetches data including official common names, geographical information, capital, area, population, languages.

title: Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
text: tibble one row containing Mexico's country information: name_common Common name country name_official Official name country region Geographic region subregion Geographic subregion capital Capital city(ies) area Total area square kilometers population Total population languages Languages spoken (comma-separated)

title: Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
text: function makes request REST Countries API v3.1 endpoint specifically Mexico using full text search. handles API errors gracefully returns NULL request fails data found.

title: Get Country Information for Mexico — get_country_info_mx
code: # \donttest{ # Get Mexico information mx_info <- get_country_info_mx() print(mx_info) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   name_common name_official region subregion capital   area population languages #>   <chr>       <chr>         <chr>  <chr>     <chr>    <dbl>      <int> <chr>     #> 1 Mexico      United Mexic… Ameri… North Am… Mexico… 1.96e6  128932753 Spanish   # }

what: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
title: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
text: Retrieves Mexico's Consumer Price Index (CPI) using 2010 base year, years 2010 2022 World Bank Open Data API. indicator used `FP.CPI.TOTL`.

title: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
text: tibble four columns: indicator Indicator name (e.g., "Consumer price index (2010 = 100)") country Country name ("Mexico") year Year data (integer) value Consumer Price Index value (numeric)

title: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
text: function sends GET request World Bank API. API request fails returns error status code, function returns `NULL` informative message.

title: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi
text: Requires internet connection. Data retrieved real time World Bank API.

title: Get Mexico's Consumer Price Index (2010 = 100) from World Bank — get_mexico_cpi

text: function sends GET request World Bank API. API request fails returns error status code, function returns `NULL` informative message.

text: Requires internet connection. data retrieved real time World Bank API.

what: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
title: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
text: Retrieves list official public holidays Mexico specific year using Nager.Date public holidays API. function returns tibble containing date holiday, name local language (Spanish), English name. useful academic, planning, data analysis purposes. information retrieved directly Nager.Date API reflects current status holidays requested year. field names returned consistent API structure.

title: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
text: Data obtained Nager.Date API: https://date.nager./

title: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
text: year integer indicating year (e.g., 2024 2025).

title: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
text: tibble following columns: date: Date holiday (class Date) local_name: Holiday name local language (Spanish) name: Holiday name English

title: Get Official Public Holidays in Mexico for a Given Year — get_mexico_holidays
code: get_mexico_holidays(2024) #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    date       local_name                              name                     #>    <date>     <chr>                                   <chr>                    #>  1 2024-01-01 Año Nuevo                               New Year's Day           #>  2 2024-02-05 Día de la Constitución                  Constitution Day         #>  3 2024-03-18 Natalicio de Benito Juárez              Benito Juárez's birthday #>  4 2024-03-28 Jueves Santo                            Maundy Thursday          #>  5 2024-03-29 Viernes Santo                           Good Friday              #>  6 2024-05-01 Día del Trabajo                         Labour Day               #>  7 2024-09-16 Día de la Independencia                 Independence Day         #>  8 2024-10-01 Transmisión del Poder Ejecutivo Federal Inauguration Day         #>  9 2024-11-18 Día de la Revolución                    Revolution Day           #> 10 2024-12-25 Navidad                                 Christmas Day            get_mexico_holidays(2025) #> # A tibble: 9 × 3 #>   date       local_name                 name                     #>   <date>     <chr>                      <chr>                    #> 1 2025-01-01 Año Nuevo                  New Year's Day           #> 2 2025-02-03 Día de la Constitución     Constitution Day         #> 3 2025-03-17 Natalicio de Benito Juárez Benito Juárez's birthday #> 4 2025-04-17 Jueves Santo               Maundy Thursday          #> 5 2025-04-18 Viernes Santo              Good Friday              #> 6 2025-05-01 Día del Trabajo            Labour Day               #> 7 2025-09-16 Día de la Independencia    Independence Day         #> 8 2025-11-17 Día de la Revolución       Revolution Day           #> 9 2025-12-25 Navidad                    Christmas Day

what: MexicoDataAPI: Access Mexican Data via APIs and Curated Datasets — MexicoDataAPI

text: package provides functions access data 'World Bank API','REST Countries API' 'Nager.Date API', covering Mexico's economic indicators, population statistics, literacy rates, international geopolitical information official public holidays. package also includes curated datasets related Mexico air quality monitoring stations, pollution zones, income surveys, postal abbreviations, election studies .

title: 2012 Mexico Elections Panel Study — mexico_elections_df
text: dataset, mexico_elections_df, data frame containing subset 2012 Mexico Elections Panel Study. dataset includes 1004 observations 25 variables, covering demographics, political preferences, attitudes toward electoral processes. provides valuable insights voter behavior perceptions 2012 federal elections Mexico.

title: 2012 Mexico Elections Panel Study — mexico_elections_df
2012 Mexico Elections Panel Study — mexico_elections_df
text: data frame 1004 observations 25 variables: mex.t Wave number (integer) mex.male Gender: 1 = Male, 0 = Female (integer) mex.age Age respondent (numeric) mex.education Level education (integer) mex.y.Year variable (integer) mex.vote Vote intention behavior (integer) mex.age2 Age squared (numeric) mex.interest Interest politics (integer) mex.married Marital status (integer) mex.pidpanw2 Partisan ID: PAN (integer) mex.pidprdw2 Partisan ID: PRD (integer) mex.pidpriw2 Partisan ID: PRI (integer) mex.votecard Possession voting card (integer) mex.urban Urban residence indicator (integer) mex.cleanelections Perception clean elections (integer) mex.metro Lives metropolitan area (integer) mex.centralregion Lives central region (integer) mex.northregion Lives northern region (integer) mex.wealth Wealth index (numeric) mex.epnapprove Approval electoral authority (integer) mex.havepropoganda Received political propaganda (integer) mex.concurrent Concurrent elections indicator (integer) mex.cleanelectionsmiss Missing response clean elections question (integer) mex.loyal Party loyalty (integer) mex.direct Direct benefit party/government (integer)

what: Pollution Measuring Stations in Mexico City — pollution_stations_df
title: Pollution Measuring Stations in Mexico City — pollution_stations_df

title: MexicoDataAPI 0.3.0
text: get_country_info_mx() removed package. ‘REST Countries API’ v3.1 endpoint depended deprecated provider. replacement v5 endpoint now requires API key, incompatible CRAN’s policy packages using Internet resources (must work without user-provided credentials). consequence, ‘REST Countries API’ longer used MexicoDataAPI.

title: MexicoDataAPI 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-13

title: MexicoDataAPI 0.2.0
text: get_country_info() renamed get_country_info_mx() avoid naming conflicts packages improve function identification. following API added MexicoDataAPI package: ‘Nager.Date’ https://date.nager./Api. following function added MexicoDataAPI package: get_mexico_holidays(): Get official public holidays Mexico given year, e.g., get_mexico_holidays(2025).
